projects.name: MICROTECT
projects.summary: An automated optical detection system for identifying microplastics in rock salt using computer vision and an embedded Raspberry Pi workflow.
projects.impact: Positions the portfolio around real engineering systems that integrate AI, physical hardware, environmental monitoring, and operator-facing software.
projects.role: Capstone team leader, full-system developer, frontend UI/UX, backend processing logic, AI workflow, and hardware integration
highlights: Detected and quantified microplastic particles using YOLOv11 and optical imaging
Integrated Raspberry Pi 5, camera module, lighting control, and real-time UI
Developed the full operator workflow from image capture and detection to frontend UI, graph views, and reports

details.problem: Manual microscopy for microplastic inspection is slow, inconsistent, and difficult to repeat across multiple rock salt samples.
details.solution: Build a compact AI-assisted inspection system that captures sample images, detects particles, visualizes findings, and supports report generation.
details.architecture: Sample tray and optical capture setup -> Raspberry Pi 5 image processing -> backend detection and measurement logic -> YOLOv11 result handling -> touchscreen frontend UI -> report and graph outputs.
details.software: YOLOv11 detection pipeline with annotated training data, OpenCV image handling, backend processing logic, bounding-box identification, pixel-to-micrometer conversion, frontend result UI, UX flow, real-time feedback, graph views, and report generation.
results: Conducted multi-trial testing across different sample sources
Computed mean detection consistency of 69.71% based on microplastic count
Model performance: precision 0.826, recall 0.733, and mAP@50 of 0.814
Reduced dependence on fully manual visual inspection
challenges: Improved optical detection through controlled imaging and illumination
Handled varied particle appearance through dataset annotation and model evaluation
Designed the UI/UX around fast inspection, visualization, result reading, and documentation

projects.posterNote: AI detection | Raspberry Pi | environmental monitoring

projects.name: ESP32-Based Remote Monitoring System
projects.summary: A real-time environmental monitoring system that combines motion, temperature, and smoke sensing with LCD-based feedback.
projects.impact: Shows embedded systems fundamentals: sensor integration, modular validation, and practical monitoring logic.
projects.role: Sensor integration, firmware logic, component testing
highlights: Integrated multiple sensors into one monitoring workflow
Displayed environmental readings through an LCD interface
Built and validated each sensor module before system integration

details.problem: Small environments need low-cost monitoring for motion, temperature changes, and smoke conditions without relying on a full commercial system.
details.solution: Use an ESP32-based controller to collect sensor readings, process environmental states, and present live status through a local display.
details.architecture: Sensor inputs -> ESP32 firmware -> threshold and status logic -> LCD output and alert-ready state.
details.software: Arduino-style firmware with modular sensor reads, threshold checks, serial debugging, and LCD status updates.
results: Produced real-time display feedback from multiple sensors
Validated each sensor component independently before combined testing
challenges: Separated component tests to isolate wiring and reading issues
Kept firmware modular so each sensor could be debugged without breaking the full system

projects.name: Smart Pet Feeder System
projects.summary: An automated feeding prototype using ultrasonic and LDR sensing, servo-controlled dispensing, buzzer alerts, and OLED status feedback.
projects.impact: Demonstrates practical automation with sensing, actuation, feedback, and user-visible system state.
projects.role: Hardware logic, sensor-actuator integration, prototype feedback flow
highlights: Used ultrasonic and light sensing to support feeding decisions
Controlled servo motors for dispensing actions
Added buzzer and OLED feedback for user awareness

details.problem: Pet feeding can be inconsistent when owners are busy or away, especially without a simple way to see system status.
details.solution: Create a sensor-assisted feeder that controls dispensing, reports status, and alerts users during operation.
details.architecture: Sensors -> microcontroller decision logic -> servo dispensing -> buzzer and OLED feedback.
details.software: Control logic for sensor readings, dispensing timing, servo movement, notification states, and OLED status messages.
results: Built an automated dispensing flow with clear local feedback
Combined sensing and actuation in a complete physical prototype
challenges: Coordinated timing between sensor reads, servo movement, and feedback
Used display and buzzer states to make the device easier to understand during testing

projects.name: HerCare
projects.summary: A care-focused companion app built for routine support, focus sessions, wellness tracking, and partner check-ins.
projects.impact: Shows product thinking, empathy in software design, frontend implementation, and the ability to turn a personal idea into a usable app.
projects.role: Product concept, frontend development, mobile UX, Android packaging
highlights: Designed around daily care routines, focus support, and simple wellness tracking
Built a clean mobile interface with reusable frontend components
Prepared the app for Android delivery using Capacitor

details.problem: Busy school and life routines can make it hard for someone to track care habits, focus sessions, and small daily reminders in one gentle place.
details.solution: Build a personal care companion app that organizes routines, wellness notes, focus tools, and partner-aware check-ins into a calm mobile experience.
details.architecture: React frontend -> local/product state -> Firebase-backed sharing features -> Capacitor Android package.
details.software: React and TypeScript interface built with Vite, packaged through Capacitor for Android, with Firebase used for app data and sharing-related flows.
results: Created a working care-focused mobile product
Practiced user-centered design through a real personal use case
Built experience with frontend architecture, state handling, and Android packaging
challenges: Kept the interface calm and simple instead of overloading it with features
Balanced emotional/personal context with practical app structure
Used AI-assisted coding and documentation support while checking implementation details

projects.name: Autonomous Line-Following and Maze-Solving Robot
projects.summary: A robot that follows lines, solves mazes with rule-based navigation, and supports Bluetooth manual override through MIT App Inventor.
projects.impact: Shows autonomous control logic, sensor-based navigation, and the ability to blend manual and automatic operation.
projects.role: Navigation logic, sensor integration, Bluetooth control flow
highlights: Implemented left-hand and right-hand rule algorithms
Used IR and ultrasonic sensors for path and obstacle awareness
Added Bluetooth control for manual override

details.problem: Autonomous robots need reliable path detection and simple recovery options when maze conditions change.
details.solution: Build a navigation robot that can follow lines, solve maze paths with rule-based algorithms, and accept manual override through Bluetooth.
details.architecture: IR and ultrasonic sensing -> navigation algorithm -> motor control -> Bluetooth manual override path.
details.software: Line-following logic, left-hand and right-hand maze rules, obstacle response, motor direction control, and MIT App Inventor command handling.
results: Supported manual control when override was needed
challenges: Balanced sensor thresholds for reliable line detection
Added Bluetooth override to recover from edge cases during testing

projects.posterNote: Maze solving | Bluetooth override | motor control

projects.name: VLAN Network Configuration
projects.summary: A Packet Tracer network configuration project using VLAN segmentation and routing for structured inter-VLAN communication.
projects.impact: Adds networking fundamentals to the portfolio and shows system-level thinking beyond embedded hardware.
projects.role: Network design, VLAN configuration, routing validation
highlights: Designed a multi-VLAN network for traffic segmentation
Configured routing for communication between VLANs
Validated connectivity in Cisco Packet Tracer

details.problem: Flat networks can become harder to manage and less secure when all devices share the same traffic domain.
details.solution: Segment the network into VLANs and configure routing so groups stay organized while still communicating when needed.
details.architecture: End devices -> access switches -> VLAN segmentation -> router or Layer 3 routing -> validated inter-VLAN traffic.
details.software: Cisco Packet Tracer configuration with VLAN assignments, switch ports, routing setup, and connectivity tests.
results: Verified communication paths between configured VLANs
challenges: Checked port assignments and routing paths during validation
Used simulation testing to confirm the network behaved as designed
